$comment: Shared config — committed to git. All machines inherit these values. Machine-specific overrides go in config/local.json (gitignored).
$comment_auto_push: Pillar 1 step 1 (worldbuilder/VEYDRIA-VISION.md). Top-level auto_push is the fleet-wide kill switch. BOTH this AND per-project auto_push (in local.json projects_in_rotation) must be true to push. auto_pr is deprecated; draft PR is implicit on push success.
$comment_overseer: Pillar 1 steps 3+4 (worldbuilder/VEYDRIA-VISION.md). Gates 5+6 of the seven-gate stack. Gate 5: read-only Overseer labels draft PRs. Gate 6: when auto_merge:true at BOTH top-level and per-repo, after cooling_off_minutes the bot flips ready and merges. Mechanism stays dormant: top-level auto_merge:false default, all repos default auto_merge:false in their object entries (or are bare strings, which are read-only). Operator opts in by setting enabled:true, listing repos with auto_merge:true objects, and flipping top-level auto_merge:true. See docs/OVERSEER.md and docs/AUTO-MERGE.md.
$comment_auto_merge: Pillar 1 step 4 -- gate 6 fleet kill switch. Top-level auto_merge:false keeps the mechanism dormant fleet-wide regardless of any per-repo opt-ins. cooling_off_minutes is the wait between overseer:approved and the bot's ready-flip; default 45. cooling_off_minutes_after_ready (default 0) is the wait between ready-flip and the merge call. post_merge_canary_replay_schedule_min is gate 7's replay cadence in MINUTES (T+15m/1h/4h/24h).
_max_runs_per_day_rationale: Per-machine daily cap, primarily a runaway/circuit-breaker for the Node engine (which uses free-tier Gemini/Mistral, not Claude Max). Real binding constraint is per-key Gemini Pro RPD (~50/day free tier) — when that's exhausted, the C2 fallback chain (DECISIONS.md 2026-04-14, commit af1071f) routes selector calls to Flash and Mistral, so dispatches still complete. The dashboard surfaces selector_fallback_count + degraded state when fallback rate climbs. 96 = one per 15 min if every cycle could fire — effectively uncapped during normal operation while still bounded enough to catch a runaway loop.
$comment_projects_in_rotation: Canonical fleet-wide project list. Each coder machine sees these on next git pull + cron tick. Per-machine overrides (e.g. different path, project disabled on one machine) live in local.json projects_in_rotation and are merged BY SLUG (see scripts/lib/config.mjs mergeProjectsBySlug). Opt-in to auto_push: set per-project auto_push:true AND populate auto_push_allowlist AND set canary_command. Top-level auto_push (above) gates the whole fleet.

projects_in_rotation.$comment: Worldbuilder Pillar 2 Phase 1 (2026-04-28). canary_command runs canon-canary which validates frontmatter id/civilization namespace + cross_refs against design/narrative-schema/canon.json. Mechanism dormant on ship: auto_push:false. Real opt-in waits on (a) sandbox-workflow-enhancement smoking 3 clean cycles, (b) hand-crafted draft PR exercising canon-canary against a real LLM diff. See worldbuilder/docs/CANON-CANARY.md.

canary_command lives here so every coder machine has it on auto-pull. Without it, gate 7 logs canary-command-missing on each pending-merges replay attempt and degraded ntfy fires (the alert that prompted promoting projects_in_rotation to shared.json).
